title: Gmelina finlaysoniana
taxonomy: Gmelina finlaysoniana is a species within the family Lamiaceae, commonly known as the mint or deadnettle family. It belongs to the genus Gmelina, which includes several other species of flowering plants. Gmelina finlaysoniana is a tropical tree native to Southeast Asia, and its classification is further divided into the kingdom Plantae and the phylum Angiospermophyta. The species is a member of the order Lamiales and the class Magnoliopsida. This classification places Gmelina finlaysoniana among other flowering plants within the angiosperm group.
uses: Gmelina finlaysoniana is known to have various medicinal uses in traditional medicine. The plant is used to treat fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues. Its leaves and bark are used to make infusions and decoctions that help alleviate symptoms. It also exhibits antimicrobial properties, which can help combat infections. Traditionally, it is used to treat various health conditions, including diarrhea and skin problems.

intro: Gmelina finlaysoniana, also known as the East Indian Beech, is a deciduous tree species native to Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory problems, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Gmelina finlaysoniana include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Gmelina finlaysoniana is commonly prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and its leaves and bark are used to make teas and infusions to alleviate symptoms associated with various health conditions.
